news.headline: Hegseth Welcomes Ford Carrier Strike Group Home After Record-Breaking Deployment
news.summary: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth was on hand with his wife, Jennifer, and numerous senior Navy leaders at Naval Station Norfolk, Va., to welcome home sailors assigned to USS Gerald R. Ford Carrier Strike Group after a record-setting, multi-month deployment.

news.headline: Félicien Kabuga Dies; Accused of Masterminding Rwanda’s Genocide
news.summary: One of his country’s richest businessmen, he fled arrest and escaped prosecution for allegedly financing and directing the bloodletting that cost 800,000 lives in 1994.

news.headline: Train Crashes Into Bangkok Traffic, Killing at Least 8
news.summary: A freight train plowed into traffic on train tracks, starting a fire. The authorities did not immediately give a cause but said a barrier had not come down.

news.headline: Two presidential campaign staffers killed in Colombia as elections near
news.summary: Rights office says the violence could hinder 'exercise of political rights' ahead of May vote to replace Gustavo Petro.

news.headline: Poll shows Lula and Bolsonaro tied before Brazil’s presidential election
news.summary: Right-wing challenger Flavio Bolsonaro faces new scrutiny over a film funding scandal, which could affect the race.

news.headline: Trump Says a Top ISIS Leader Was Killed in a U.S.-Nigerian Mission
news.summary: Abu Bilal al-Minuki, said to be the Islamic State’s second-ranking leader, was killed after a helicopter-borne assault by commandos from both countries on Friday night, two U.S. officials said.

news.headline: Taiwan says it is an independent nation after Trump arms sale remarks
news.summary: Taiwan stressed that it is a “sovereign and independent” nation

news.headline: FIFA says held positive talks with Iran ahead of World Cup
news.summary: FIFA Secretary General Mattias Grafstrom said Saturday he held a constructive meeting with the president of Iran's FA Mehdi Taj regarding Iran’s participation in the World Cup which is set to take place this summer in the United States, Canada, and Mexico.

news.headline: Bolivian army attempts to clear roads after 11 days of protests
news.summary: Military police in Bolivia arrested demonstrators, and used tear gas to disrupt road blockades after 11 days of protests

news.headline: Canadian from hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests positive
news.summary: The individual is one of four former passengers on the MV Hondius isolating on Vancouver Island, British Columbia.

news.headline: Man drives car into pedestrians in Italy, injuring eight
news.summary: Eight people were injured, four seriously, before passers-by gave chase and stopped the man.

news.headline: Thousands protest at Eurovision final as five countries boycott over Israel
news.summary: Spain, Ireland, Iceland, Slovenia and Netherlands all withdrew in protest against Israel's participation amid Gaza war.
